title: Foot per second
lede:
The foot per second (plural feet per second) is a unit of both speed (scalar) and velocity (vector quantity, which includes direction). It expresses the distance in feet (ft) traveled or displaced, divided by the time in seconds (s). 
The corresponding unit in the International System of Units (SI) is the meter per second.
Abbreviations include ft/s, fps, and the scientific notation ft s−1.
sections:
The foot per second (plural feet per second) is a unit of both speed (scalar) and velocity (vector quantity, which includes direction). It expresses the distance in feet (ft) traveled or displaced, divided by the time in seconds (s). 
The corresponding unit in the International System of Units (SI) is the meter per second.
Abbreviations include ft/s, fps, and the scientific notation ft s−1.

== See also ==
Foot per second squared, a corresponding unit of acceleration.
Feet per minute

title: Foot per second squared
lede:
The foot per second squared (plural feet per second squared) is a unit of acceleration. It expresses change in velocity expressed in units of feet per second (ft/s) divided by time in seconds (s) (or the distance in feet (ft) traveled or displaced, divided by the time in seconds (s) squared). The corresponding unit in the International System of Units (SI) is the metre per second squared.
Abbreviations include ft/s2, ft/sec2, ft/s/s, ft/sec/sec, and ft s−2.
sections:
The foot per second squared (plural feet per second squared) is a unit of acceleration. It expresses change in velocity expressed in units of feet per second (ft/s) divided by time in seconds (s) (or the distance in feet (ft) traveled or displaced, divided by the time in seconds (s) squared). The corresponding unit in the International System of Units (SI) is the metre per second squared.
Abbreviations include ft/s2, ft/sec2, ft/s/s, ft/sec/sec, and ft s−2.

== See also ==

Metre per second squared
